rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARY GRACE KIMBROUGH FIRST PARTY SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The judicial officer is to review and determine whether to take this petition off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 05/23/2025): 1) If petitioners are requesting authority to pay up to $2,800 to their attorney as fees on account, the information required by Probate Code section 2643(c)(applicable to court-supervised special needs trusts under LRSF 14.48(A)) must be provided. Otherwise, please file copies of additional invoices describing legal services rendered after 12/17/2024. 2) Schedule of Disbursements does not show payment of $3,260 to the attorney approved by the 07/05/2023 order issued in the related conservatorship case. Was the attorney paid from the trust after this account period? 3) The original payee must be disclosed re the following disbursements labeled as reimbursements (see LRSF 14.34(B)): i) $43.59 paid to Sara Freeman on 01/18/2024; ii) $25.52 paid to Jill Kimbrough on 02/12/2024; iii) $51.36 paid to Jill Kimbrough on 04/12/2024; and iv) $168.70 paid to Jill Kimbrough on 05/20/2024. 4) Did the trust pay 100% of "house cleaning" expenses ($4,204.60 in total)? If so, please explain why other members of the same household should not be required to contribute. 5) The attorney invoices include legal services predating the 07/05/2023 order that established the trust and appointed petitioners as trustees. The 07/05/2023 order approved the attorney fees of $40,840 "for legal services rendered and costs advanced in connection with the establishment and administration of the SNT". The 07/05/2023 order also approved $3,260 for "legal services provided from 11/14/22 -11/28/22", but the attorney invoices include services provided during that period ($3,120 in total). 6) Court fees previously waived in the related conservatorship case have not been reimbursed from the trust. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARNOLD YEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/25/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The amended petition was just filed on 9/29/25 (e-filed on 9/30/25), which was not at least 15 days before this hearing date. (2) The proof of service filed on 8/29/25 is the improper form of proof of service. The only correct form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing (DE-120) that was served must be attached to it. (3) Paragraph 1 of the amended petition does not make sense. The decedent was reported to have died on 5/23/22, and it is unclear why the residence of the executor is mentioned. (4) Paragraph 8 of the amended petition is inaccurate. The administrator filed her Partial #1 I&A on 5/22/24, showing $2,375,000 and her Partial #2/Final on 1/16/25, showing $7,000. Executor's I&A filed on 5/15/25 showed just the $2,375,000. (5) The Summary of Account in paragraph 10 of the amended petition is incomplete. If there was $2,381,317.75 on hand as of 1/17/25, and disbursements totaled $224,692.38, why is there just $1,931,625.37 on hand? (That Summary of Account also does not agree with the actual account attached to the amended petition.) (6) The two remaining debts Decedent listed in his "Final Arrangements" and referenced in paragraph of the amended petition must be paid before the estate can close. (7) The "accounting" indicates that none of the claims and costs have actually been paid yet. All costs that should have been disbursed from the estate are shown on the liabilities schedule. (8) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (9) The statutory fee shown in paragraphs 21, 25, and 26 of the amended petition and in paragraph 2 of the prayer does not agree with the amount shown on the attached Statutory Fee Calculation. (10) There was no need to serve notice of proposed action; the executor is the sole beneficiary. (11) The amount of the fees for extraordinary services is missing from the prayer. (12) The proposed order also does not correspond to the prayer in the amended petition. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/10/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The will was admitted on 12/24/24, not 12/24/25. Only the FTB filed a Request for Special Notice and later withdrew it. Dealing with creditor claims is ordinary; however, since the executor is the sole beneficiary, the Court will give weight to her request. The exhibits were out of order, making review a little challenging.) NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE JESUS O. PULE AND AMALIA PULE LIVING TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition (1) For A Report Of The Trust And Its Administration; 2) For An Accounting 3) For An Order To Distribute Trust Assets; 4) For Damages Due To
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/26/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proof of service of notice has been filed. (Only beneficiaries of the operative trust need to be served notice.) (2) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Notice must be mailed directly to the person entitled to notice. A notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person.[CRC 7.51(a).] Please provide a copy of the proof of service of Notice of Hearing of this petition to the assigned examiner by 11/12/25. (A copy of the "Answer" filed on 8/4/25 has not been received yet by the assigned examiner.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues that remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. In a contested matter, a copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled to notice. Proof of service must be filed. LRSF 14.7(A)(4). 2) The DE-147S form on file did not list petitioner's driver's license number. 3) Item 1 of the DE-121 form on file did not list the decedent's aka (Urbina - see proof of publication). 4) Item 2d(2) of the petition: Absent waivers of bond signed by all other beneficiaries, the bond amount must match the total value alleged at item 3d(7) rounded up to the nearest thousand dollars ($1,884,000). 5) Item 3g(2)(c) must be checked with petitioner's relationship to the decedent specified. 6) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents. 7) If petitioner's status as the decedent's surviving spouse remains in dispute, please provide proof of petitioner's marriage to the decedent. 8) No competing petition has been filed by the objector to request appointment of another person as administrator. See Probate Code section 8004(a). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHRYN E BASS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/26/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Item 2b of the petition is incomplete. (2) Item 3c of the petition appears to be an incomplete address. (3) Item 4 of the petition was checked, when there is reportedly no will. (4) Item 7 of the petition must be completed if no spouse or issue survived the decedent. A copy of the predeceased spouse's death certificate may need to be filed to verify his date of death. (5) The proposed personal representative is missing from item 8 of the petition. (6) Has Nick Tarlson consented to serve? His consent was not attached to the petition and he was not given notice. (7) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/12/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF LISA MAGRUDER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues have not been cleared: (1) Declaration [BMD-002A] must be completed. Item #3 is missing the names of the persons who were married on 7/24/2023. (2) Attachment 4, titled "SF City Hall Events," appears to be a signed contract for a future event (wedding) using the City building. This attachment alone does not establish the fact of the marriage. A verified supplemental declaration providing factual support must be filed by the petitioner. (3) The verified supplemental declaration may include details about the marriage license, such as whether it had been issued and/or delivered back to the office, and so on. (4) If applicable, a written affidavit from the officiant of the marriage, attendees, or anyone else who can attest to the validity of the marriage should also be submitted. (5) Proposed Order (Page 1 only) must be submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
